content_en:
One of the wives of the Mujahideen said:

“I have heard my father saying that if poison is cooked, then the effect of the poison is neutralised.”

She then picked some grains from the earthen pot and put them in one of their pans and put it on the fire. Then she pointed out that the husk was coming off from the grains, and white grains were appearing. Just to check out the taste we put them in bowl and gave them to the general. He told us to try them, taking the Name of Allah 3, and when he had tasted them he found them to be delicious. Later we learnt these grains were called rice and were the favourite food of Iranians.”

Ablah which ‘Utbah bin Ghazwan - had attacked with a small army was situated on the banks of the river Dajlah. It was a very secure city where the Iranians had built huge storehouses to safely stock their weapons. On the ramparts of the forts they had constructed towers to keep a watch on any approaching enemy. But none of these measures could impede the march of ‘Utbah bin Ghazwan 4 and his army.

‘Utbah bin Ghazwan covered the spears in a decorative manner with some flags and gave them in the hands of the women and ordered them to walk behind the army. And he told them that when they neared the city they should stir up a lot of dust; the hazy atmosphere would thus reduce visibility. When they neared the city the Iranian army was aghast to see such a huge army advancing towards them. Their numbers were few and they were terrified for their lives. In sheer panic they jumped into the boats moored for emergencies on the river Dajlahh, and fled. ‘Utbah bin Ghazwan 4s, without any hindrance or opposition entered Ablah triumphantly. A short time later he conquered quite easily the surrounding towns and settlements. Here the army of {slam gained immense spoils, the value of which was difficult to assess.

After their success when one of the Mujahid returned to Al-Madinah and was asked how the Muslims were doing there. He replied that they were fine and enjoying the comfortable life greatly. People in fact were keen to settle in Ablah permanently!

‘Utbah bin Ghazwan 4% thought that if the Muslims continued to stay for some time in the conquered cities and towns they would become lazy and begin to enjoy a soft life of idleness, ease and comfort. The culture, habits and customs of the conquered people would also influence them. Besides, their toughness and their resolve to do Jihad in the way of Islam would weaken. So he wrote a letter to Caliph ‘Umar Farooq -# requesting his permission to found a new city; he also pinpointed the site he had selected. ‘Umar Farooq % granted permission and the name proposed for the new city was Basra.

‘Utbah bin Ghazwan 4 drew up the plan for the new city. Firsta huge mosque was constructed. It became the centre for all the work

‘Utbah bin Ghazwan 4% was not only a successful General but also a simple and humble man who was knownas a reformer as well. He realised that the Muslims who had settled in Basra had begun to care too deeply for things of this world; this love of luxuries could make person neglect the life of the spirit and of the Hereafter. He saw that the people who did not know even the word rice had developed a taste for Iranian delicacies. When he realised that the spiritual values of Islam were being endangered by the life of this world he gathered the people in the mosque and addressed them thus:

“O People! The life of this world is going to end. You will be transferred from here to a house, which will be yours forever. You will take with you only your good deeds. People, I have had the distinction of being among the first to accept Islam. At that time we used to eat the leaves of trees to survive and sometimes our mouths used to be full of sores and wounds because of this. Our poverty was such that often we had difficulty finding clothes to cover our bodies. One day I found a sheet, which I cut up into two. One I used to cover myself and the other I gave to Sa’d_ bin Abu Wags to use as a loin cloth. Today all of us are governors of some province or the other. I ask for Allah protection from the condition where I may think myself to be very great now, but in the sight of Allah I shall be considered very low.”

he had placed him in the pre-eminent position of the caliphate and wanted to escape himself. He insisted that he should help him. He obeyed the command of Caliph ‘Umar Faroog very reluctantly and getting on to his camel he rode back to Basra. On the way out of AlMadinah he kept on praying earnestly, “O Allah please don’t take me back! O Allah please don’t take me back!”

Allah 3% accepted his supplications and he had just gone a little distance from of Al-Madinah, when his camel stumbled. ‘Utbah bin Ghazwan # fell died instantly and went on to meet his Creature.

“Come back to your Lord - well-pleased (yourself) and well pleasing (unto Him)!” (89:28)

All the Quraish set off for Mountain Uhud to fight against the Prophet #. Adults and children, young and old, rich and poor - everyone was there. Because of their complete rout at the Battle of Badr, their hearts were filled with fury and rage against the Muslims. They were eager to avenge the death of their near and dear ones, and the fire of revenge burnt bright as they wanted desperately to erase the Muslims from the face of the earth. They had taken their women along with them so that they could inspire the men and remind the youth of the great deeds of their forefathers and inspire them to perform heroic feats and not retreat. Among these women was the wife of Abu Sufyan, Hind bin ‘Utbah and Reetah the wife of ‘Amr bin ‘Aas. The wife of Talah, Salafah bint Sa’d had joined them with her three sons Musaffa’, Jalas and Kalab. Besides these there were many others who accompanied the army.

When fierce fighting broke out between the two armies in the Battle of Uhud, then Hind and the other women went and stood behind the line of the warriors; beating heir drums they started to sing:

If you advance we will embrace you, and prop up pillows for you. If you turn your backs and flee, we will spurn you. (poetry)

The emotional anthems inspired emotions of tribal pride and worked like magic on their husbands.

Then the battle seemed to cool down and the Quraish dominated over the Muslims. The women, intoxicated with the joy of victory, came out on the battlefield and started to dance and desecrate the dead bodies of the Mujahideen. In the passionate desire for revenge, they slit up

their bellies, gouged out their eyes and cut off their noses and ears. One woman, her appetite for revenge for the men of her family not satisfied with all this, strung the noses and ears she had cut off ona piece of string and wore it as a necklace! On another string she shaped an anklet and put it on her feet and started dancing joyfully amidst the dead bodies.

But Salafah bint Sa‘d was very different from her friends. She was anxiously expecting her family - her husband and three sons - to turn up any moment; once she saw them she would also join the dancers in the celebrations of victory, she thought. Finally she got tired, her long wait seemed wasted and she started looking for the faces of her dear ones among the dead. She finally found her husband's dead body soaked in blood. She turned around like an enraged tigress and started looking swiftly in every corner of the battlefield for her three sons. Some time later she found the three bodies scattered in the field under Mountain Uhud. Muséaffa’ and Kalab were dead and her third son, Jalas was breathing his last.

Stricken with grief she sat down beside him, took his head on her lap and started cleaning up his face and his forehead. She was trying her utmost to save his life; she was heartbroken but could not shed a tear. In deep sorrow she asked her son:

“My dear son, who has given you these grievous wounds?”

He wanted to reply, but could not as he was panting for life. Again and again she repeated her question. Finally, he gasped out:

“My father, brothers and | all killed by ‘Asim bin Thabit 4.”

Hearing this Salafah started to scream and swore by the false gods ‘Uzza and Lat that her rage could not die down until she had taken revenge on him, and drank wine from his skull.

Then she proclaimed that she would give any amount of wealth desired, to the person, who brought him alive or brought his head. The news of this announcement of hers spread like wildfire among the Quraish. All the young men started to wish that this wealth should be

theirs and they should be the lucky ones to succeed in killing ‘Asim bin Thabit &.

The Muslims returned to Al-Madinah after the Battle of Uhud and started discussing its outcome amongst them. They offered supplications to Allah for the Mujahideen and spoke respectfully of their courage and daring. They were surprised that ‘Asim bin Thabit .& had killed four members of the same family.

One of them said:
content_id:
Salah seorang istri para Mujahidin berkata:

"Aku pernah mendengar ayahku berkata bahwa jika racun dimasak, maka efek racunnya akan hilang."

Ia kemudian mengambil beberapa butir biji-bijian dari periuk tanah liat dan menaruhnya di salah satu wajan mereka lalu meletakkannya di atas api. Kemudian ia menunjukkan bahwa sekam mulai terkelupas dari biji-bijian tersebut, dan butiran putih mulai tampak. Untuk mencicipi rasanya, kami menaruhnya di mangkuk dan memberikannya kepada jenderal. Ia menyuruh kami mencobanya dengan menyebut Nama Allah ﷻ, dan ketika ia mencicipinya, ia mendapati rasanya sangat lezat. Kemudian kami mengetahui bahwa biji-bijian itu disebut beras dan merupakan makanan kesukaan orang-orang Iran."

Ablah yang diserang oleh 'Utbah bin Ghazwan رضي الله عنه dengan pasukan kecil terletak di tepi sungai Dajlah. Kota itu sangat aman, di mana orang-orang Iran telah membangun gudang-gudang besar untuk menyimpan senjata mereka dengan aman. Di atas benteng mereka telah membangun menara-menara pengawas untuk memantau musuh yang mendekat. Namun semua langkah tersebut tidak mampu menghalangi gerak maju 'Utbah bin Ghazwan رضي الله عنه dan pasukannya.

'Utbah bin Ghazwan menghiasi tombak-tombak dengan beberapa bendera dan memberikannya ke tangan para wanita serta memerintahkan mereka berjalan di belakang pasukan. Ia menyuruh mereka agar ketika mendekati kota, mereka mengepulkan debu sebanyak mungkin; suasana berkabut akan mengurangi jarak pandang. Ketika mereka mendekati kota, pasukan Iran terkejut melihat pasukan yang begitu besar bergerak ke arah mereka. Jumlah mereka sedikit dan mereka ketakutan akan nyawa mereka. Dalam kepanikan luar biasa, mereka melompat ke perahu-perahu yang ditambatkan untuk keadaan darurat di sungai Dajlah, lalu melarikan diri. 'Utbah bin Ghazwan رضي الله عنه tanpa hambatan atau perlawanan memasuki Ablah dengan penuh kemenangan. Tidak lama kemudian ia dengan mudah menaklukkan kota-kota dan pemukiman di sekitarnya. Di sini pasukan Islam memperoleh rampasan perang yang sangat banyak, yang nilainya sulit untuk ditaksir.

Setelah kemenangan mereka, ketika salah seorang Mujahid kembali ke Al-Madinah dan ditanya bagaimana keadaan kaum Muslimin di sana, ia menjawab bahwa mereka baik-baik saja dan sangat menikmati kehidupan yang nyaman. Bahkan banyak orang yang ingin menetap di Ablah secara permanen!

'Utbah bin Ghazwan رضي الله عنه berpikir bahwa jika kaum Muslimin terus tinggal beberapa lama di kota-kota yang ditaklukkan, mereka akan menjadi malas dan mulai menikmati kehidupan yang lembek, santai dan nyaman. Budaya, kebiasaan dan adat istiadat penduduk yang ditaklukkan juga akan mempengaruhi mereka. Selain itu, ketangguhan dan tekad mereka untuk berjihad di jalan Islam akan melemah. Maka ia menulis surat kepada Khalifah 'Umar Farooq رضي الله عنه meminta izin untuk mendirikan sebuah kota baru; ia juga menunjukkan lokasi yang telah dipilihnya. 'Umar Farooq رضي الله عنه memberikan izin dan nama yang diusulkan untuk kota baru itu adalah Basra.

'Utbah bin Ghazwan رضي الله عنه merancang rencana untuk kota baru tersebut. Pertama-tama sebuah masjid besar dibangun. Masjid itu menjadi pusat segala kegiatan.

'Utbah bin Ghazwan رضي الله عنه bukan hanya seorang jenderal yang sukses tetapi juga seorang yang sederhana dan rendah hati yang dikenal sebagai seorang pembaharu. Ia menyadari bahwa kaum Muslimin yang telah menetap di Basra mulai terlalu mencintai hal-hal duniawi; kecintaan terhadap kemewahan ini bisa membuat seseorang mengabaikan kehidupan rohani dan akhirat. Ia melihat bahwa orang-orang yang bahkan tidak mengenal kata beras telah mengembangkan selera terhadap kelezatan makanan Iran. Ketika ia menyadari bahwa nilai-nilai spiritual Islam terancam oleh kehidupan duniawi, ia mengumpulkan orang-orang di masjid dan berkhutbah kepada mereka:

"Wahai manusia! Kehidupan dunia ini akan berakhir. Kalian akan dipindahkan dari sini ke sebuah tempat tinggal yang akan menjadi milik kalian selamanya. Kalian hanya akan membawa amal baik kalian. Wahai manusia, aku memiliki kehormatan sebagai salah satu orang pertama yang masuk Islam. Pada waktu itu kami biasa memakan daun-daun pohon untuk bertahan hidup dan kadang-kadang mulut kami penuh dengan luka dan borok karenanya. Kemiskinan kami sedemikian rupa sehingga seringkali kami kesulitan menemukan pakaian untuk menutupi tubuh kami. Suatu hari aku menemukan selembar kain, yang kupotong menjadi dua. Satu kugunakan untuk menutupi diriku dan yang lain kuberikan kepada Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas untuk digunakan sebagai kain pinggang. Hari ini kita semua adalah gubernur di suatu provinsi atau lainnya. Aku memohon perlindungan Allah dari keadaan di mana aku menganggap diriku sangat hebat sekarang, namun di sisi Allah aku dianggap sangat rendah."

Allah telah menempatkannya pada kedudukan tertinggi kekhalifahan dan ia ingin melepaskan diri dari tanggung jawabnya. Khalifah bersikeras agar ia membantunya. Ia mematuhi perintah Khalifah 'Umar Farooq dengan sangat berat hati dan naik ke atas untanya lalu berkendara kembali ke Basra. Dalam perjalanan keluar dari Al-Madinah ia terus berdoa dengan sungguh-sungguh, "Ya Allah, jangan kembalikan aku! Ya Allah, jangan kembalikan aku!"

Allah ﷻ mengabulkan doanya dan ia baru saja menempuh jarak yang sedikit dari Al-Madinah, ketika untanya tersandung. 'Utbah bin Ghazwan رضي الله عنه jatuh dan wafat seketika, pergi menemui Penciptanya.

"Kembalilah kepada Tuhanmu dengan hati yang ridha lagi diridhai-Nya!" (QS. Al-Fajr: 28)

Seluruh kaum Quraisy berangkat menuju Gunung Uhud untuk berperang melawan Nabi ﷺ. Dewasa dan anak-anak, muda dan tua, kaya dan miskin — semua ada di sana. Karena kekalahan total mereka dalam Perang Badar, hati mereka dipenuhi amarah dan kemarahan terhadap kaum Muslimin. Mereka sangat ingin membalas kematian orang-orang terdekat mereka, dan api dendam menyala terang karena mereka sangat ingin menghapuskan kaum Muslimin dari muka bumi. Mereka membawa para wanita bersama mereka agar para wanita itu dapat menginspirasi para pria dan mengingatkan para pemuda akan kepahlawanan nenek moyang mereka serta mendorong mereka untuk melakukan perbuatan heroik dan tidak mundur. Di antara para wanita itu ada istri Abu Sufyan, Hind binti 'Utbah, dan Reetah istri 'Amr bin 'Aas. Istri Talhah, Salafah binti Sa'd, bergabung bersama tiga putranya Musaffa', Jalas dan Kalab. Selain mereka masih banyak lagi yang menyertai pasukan.

Ketika pertempuran sengit pecah antara kedua pasukan dalam Perang Uhud, Hind dan para wanita lainnya pergi dan berdiri di belakang barisan para prajurit; sambil memukul gendang mereka mulai bernyanyi:

Jika kalian maju, kami akan memeluk kalian, dan menggelarkan bantal-bantal untuk kalian. Jika kalian berbalik dan melarikan diri, kami akan mencampakkan kalian. (syair)

Nyanyian penuh emosi itu membangkitkan kebanggaan kesukuan dan bekerja bagaikan sihir pada suami-suami mereka.

Kemudian pertempuran mulai mereda dan kaum Quraisy mendominasi kaum Muslimin. Para wanita, mabuk dengan kegembiraan kemenangan, keluar ke medan perang dan mulai menari serta menodai jasad para Mujahidin yang gugur. Dalam hasrat balas dendam yang membara, mereka membelah

perut mereka, mencungkil mata mereka, dan memotong hidung serta telinga mereka. Seorang wanita, yang hasrat balas dendamnya atas kematian keluarganya belum terpuaskan dengan semua itu, merangkai hidung dan telinga yang dipotongnya pada seutas tali dan memakainya sebagai kalung! Pada tali lain ia membentuk gelang kaki dan memakainya di kakinya lalu mulai menari dengan gembira di antara jasad-jasad yang tergeletak.

Namun Salafah binti Sa'd sangat berbeda dari teman-temannya. Ia dengan cemas menunggu keluarganya — suami dan tiga putranya — muncul kapan saja; begitu ia melihat mereka, ia juga akan bergabung dengan para penari dalam perayaan kemenangan, pikirnya. Akhirnya ia merasa lelah, penantian panjangnya terasa sia-sia dan ia mulai mencari wajah-wajah orang-orang tersayangnya di antara para korban. Ia akhirnya menemukan jasad suaminya bersimbah darah. Ia berbalik bagaikan harimau betina yang mengamuk dan mulai mencari dengan cepat ke setiap sudut medan perang mencari tiga putranya. Beberapa waktu kemudian ia menemukan tiga jasad yang terserak di medan di bawah Gunung Uhud. Musaffa' dan Kalab telah gugur, dan putra ketiganya, Jalas, sedang menghembuskan nafas terakhirnya.

Dilanda kesedihan mendalam, ia duduk di sampingnya, meletakkan kepalanya di pangkuannya dan mulai membersihkan wajah dan dahinya. Ia berusaha sekuat tenaga untuk menyelamatkan nyawanya; hatinya hancur namun tidak bisa meneteskan air mata. Dalam kesedihan yang mendalam ia bertanya kepada putranya:

"Anakku sayang, siapa yang memberikanmu luka-luka yang berat ini?"

Ia ingin menjawab, namun tidak bisa karena ia terengah-engah memperjuangkan hidupnya. Berkali-kali ia mengulang pertanyaannya. Akhirnya, dengan tersendat-sendat ia berkata:

"Ayah, saudara-saudaraku dan aku semua dibunuh oleh 'Asim bin Thabit رضي الله عنه."

Mendengar ini, Salafah mulai menjerit dan bersumpah demi berhala palsu 'Uzza dan Lat bahwa amarahnya tidak akan padam sampai ia membalas dendam kepadanya, dan meminum khamr dari tengkoraknya.

Kemudian dia mengumumkan bahwa dia akan memberikan sejumlah harta berapapun yang diinginkan, kepada orang yang membawanya hidup-hidup atau membawa kepalanya. Berita pengumuman ini menyebar seperti api di kalangan Quraisy. Semua pemuda mulai berharap bahwa kekayaan ini akan menjadi

milik mereka dan merekalah orang-orang yang beruntung berhasil membunuh 'Asim bin Tsabit &.

Kaum Muslimin kembali ke Madinah setelah Perang Uhud dan mulai mendiskusikan hasilnya di antara mereka. Mereka memanjatkan du'a kepada Allah untuk para Mujahidin dan berbicara dengan penuh hormat tentang keberanian dan keberanian mereka. Mereka terkejut bahwa 'Asim bin Tsabit & telah membunuh empat anggota dari keluarga yang sama.

Salah seorang dari mereka berkata:
content_ar: One of the wives of the المجاهدين said: “I have heard my الأب saying that إذا poison is cooked, then the effect of the poison is neutralised.” She then picked some grains from the earthen pot and put them in one of their pans and put it on the fire. Then she pointed out that the husk was coming off from the grains, and white grains were appearing. Just to check out the taste we put them in bowl and gave them to the general. He told us to try them, taking the Name of الله 3, and عندما he had tasted them he found them to be delicious. Later we learnt these grains were called rice and were the favourite food of Iranians.” Ablah which ‘Utbah bin Ghazwan - had attacked with a small الجيش was situated on the banks of the river Dajlah. It was a very secure city where the Iranians had built huge storehouses to safely stock their weapons. On the ramparts of the forts they had constructed towers to keep a watch on any approaching العدو. But none of these measures could impede the march of ‘Utbah bin Ghazwan 4 and his الجيش. ‘Utbah bin Ghazwan covered the spears in a decorative manner with some flags and gave them in the hands of the women and ordered them to walk behind the الجيش. And he told them that عندما they neared the city they should stir up a lot of dust; the hazy atmosphere would وهكذا reduce visibility. عندما they neared the city the Iranian الجيش was aghast to see such a huge الجيش advancing towards them. Their numbers were few and they were terrified for their lives. In sheer panic they jumped into the boats moored for emergencies on the river Dajlahh, and fled. ‘Utbah bin Ghazwan 4s, without any hindrance or opposition entered Ablah triumphantly. A short time later he conquered quite easily the surrounding towns and settlements. Here the الجيش of {slam gained immense spoils, the value of which was difficult to assess. بعد their الفلاح عندما one of the Mujahid returned to Al-المدينة المنورة and was asked how the المسلمون were doing there. أجاب that they were fine and enjoying the comfortable الحياة greatly. People in fact were keen to settle in Ablah permanently! ‘Utbah bin Ghazwan 4% thought that إذا the المسلمون continued to stay for some time in the conquered cities and towns they would become lazy and begin to enjoy a soft الحياة of idleness, اليسر and comfort. The culture, habits and customs of the conquered people would أيضاً influence them. بالإضافة إلى, their toughness and their resolve to do الجهاد in the الطريق of الإسلام would weaken. لذا كتب a letter to Caliph ‘عمر Farooq -# requesting his permission to found a new city; he أيضاً pinpointed the site he had selected. ‘عمر Farooq % granted permission and the name proposed for the new city was Basra. ‘Utbah bin Ghazwan 4 drew up the plan for the new city. Firsta huge المسجد was constructed. It became the centre for all the work ‘Utbah bin Ghazwan 4% was not only a successful General but أيضاً a simple and humble man who was knownas a reformer as well. He realised that the المسلمون who had settled in Basra had begun to care أيضاً deeply for things of this الدنيا; this المحبة of luxuries could make person neglect the الحياة of the الروح and of الآخرة. He saw that the people who did not know even the word rice had developed a taste for Iranian delicacies. عندما he realised that the spiritual values of الإسلام were being endangered by the الحياة of this الدنيا he gathered the people in the المسجد and addressed them وهكذا: “O People! The الحياة of this الدنيا is going to end. You will be transferred from here to a house, which will be yours forever. You will take with you only your الخير deeds. People, I have had the distinction of being among the أولاً to accept الإسلام. At that time we used to eat the leaves of trees to survive and sometimes our mouths used to be full of sores and wounds لأن of this. Our الفقر was such that often we had difficulty finding clothes to cover our bodies. يوماً ما I found a sheet, which I cut up into two. One I used to cover myself and the other I gave to Sa’d_ bin Abu Wags to use as a loin cloth. Today all of us are governors of some province or the other. I ask for الله protection from the condition where I may think myself to be very great now, but in the sight of الله I shall be considered very low.” he had placed him in the pre-eminent position of the الخلافة and wanted to escape himself. He insisted that he should help him. He obeyed the command of Caliph ‘عمر Faroog very reluctantly and getting on to his camel he rode back to Basra. On the الطريق out of AlMadinah he kept on praying earnestly, “O الله please don’t take me back! O الله please don’t take me back!” الله 3% accepted his supplications and he had just gone a little distance from of Al-المدينة المنورة, عندما his camel stumbled. ‘Utbah bin Ghazwan # fell died instantly and went on to meet his Creature. “Come back to your Lord - well-pleased (yourself) and well pleasing (unto Him)!” (89:28) All the Quraish set off for Mountain Uhud to fight against النبي ﷺ #. Adults and الأولاد, young and old, rich and poor - everyone was there. لأن of their complete rout at the المعركة of Badr, their القلوب were filled with fury and rage against the المسلمون. They were eager to avenge the الموت of their near and dear ones, and the fire of revenge burnt bright as they wanted desperately to erase the المسلمون from the face of the earth. They had taken their women along with them لذا that they could inspire the men and remind the youth of the great deeds of their forefathers and inspire them to perform heroic feats and not retreat. Among these women was the الزوجة of Abu Sufyan, Hind bin ‘Utbah and Reetah the الزوجة of ‘Amr bin ‘Aas. The الزوجة of Talah, Salafah bint Sa’d had joined them with her three sons Musaffa’, Jalas and Kalab. بالإضافة إلى these there were many others who accompanied the الجيش. عندما fierce fighting broke out between the two armies in the المعركة of Uhud, then Hind and the other women went and stood behind the line of the warriors; beating heir drums they started to sing: إذا you advance we will embrace you, and prop up pillows for you. إذا you turn your backs and flee, we will spurn you. (poetry) The emotional anthems inspired emotions of tribal الكبر and worked like magic on their husbands. Then the المعركة seemed to cool down and the Quraish dominated over the المسلمون. The women, intoxicated with the joy of النصر, came out on the المعركةfield and started to dance and desecrate the dead bodies of the المجاهدين. In the passionate desire for revenge, they slit up their bellies, gouged out their eyes and cut off their noses and ears. One woman, her appetite for revenge for the men of her الأسرة not satisfied with all this, strung the noses and ears she had cut off ona piece of string and wore it as a necklace! On another string she shaped an anklet and put it on her feet and started dancing joyfully amidst the dead bodies. But Salafah bint Sa‘d was very different from her friends. She was anxiously expecting her الأسرة - her الزوج and three sons - to turn up any moment; حين she saw them she would أيضاً join the dancers in the celebrations of النصر, she thought. وأخيراً she got tired, her long wait seemed wasted and she started looking for the faces of her dear ones among the dead. She وأخيراً found her الزوج's dead الجسد soaked in blood. She turned around like an enraged tigress and started looking swiftly in every corner of the المعركةfield for her three sons. Some time later she found the three bodies scattered in the field under Mountain Uhud. Muséaffa’ and Kalab were dead and her ثالثاً son, Jalas was breathing his last. Stricken with الحزن she sat down beside him, took his head on her lap and started cleaning up his face and his forehead. She was trying her utmost to save his الحياة; she was heartbroken but could not shed a tear. In deep sorrow she asked her son: “My dear son, who has given you these grievous wounds?” He wanted to reply, but could not as he was panting for الحياة. Again and again she repeated her question. وأخيراً, he gasped out: “My الأب, brothers and | all killed by ‘Asim bin Thabit 4.” Hearing this Salafah started to scream and swore by the false gods ‘Uzza and Lat that her rage could not die down حتى she had taken revenge on him, and drank wine from his skull. Then she proclaimed that she would give any amount of الثروة desired, to the person, who brought him alive or brought his head. The news of this announcement of hers spread like wildfire among the Quraish. All the young men started to wish that this الثروة should be theirs and they should be the lucky ones to succeed in killing ‘Asim bin Thabit &. The المسلمون returned to Al-المدينة المنورة بعد the المعركة of Uhud and started discussing its outcome amongst them. They offered supplications to الله for the المجاهدين and spoke respectfully of their الشجاعة and daring. They were surprised that ‘Asim bin Thabit .& had killed four members of the same الأسرة.
